notes.anchor: he was the leading man of the equestrian order
notes.note: The equestrian order (ordo equester) was the wealthy, non-senatorial "knights"; its princeps (leading man) was an informal but real position of influence. Lamia held it when he campaigned for Cicero's recall in 58 BC.

notes.anchor: he was banished from the city by the consul Gabinius
notes.note: A. Gabinius, consul 58 BC and an ally of Clodius, ordered Lamia out of Rome for agitating on Cicero's behalf --- an extraordinary act, since (Cicero notes) no Roman citizen had been relegated from the city itself before this.

notes.anchor: with that most magnificent aedile's show to his credit
notes.note: As aedile (probably 45 BC) Lamia had staged exceptionally lavish public games (munus), the customary way to win popular favour before standing for the praetorship.

notes.anchor: since the centuries of the knights are in your hand --- you are king there
notes.note: Praetors were elected by the comitia centuriata, voting by centuries; the eighteen equestrian centuries carried special weight. Cicero asks Decimus Brutus, as the knights' patron, to deliver their votes for Lamia.
